promotionQueue.title: Audit Trail on LFForms
promotionQueue.snippet: n. But no matter how I try to format the URL of the server that is running Audit Trail (it is the same server running LFServer and LFDS), it just keeps giving me errors like this: Unable to connect to Audit Trail at 'https://xxx.xxxxxxxx.xxx' [LFF9900-AuditTrailConnectionFailed] I've read through the help files about the configuration , and I think I've addressed everything listed there. Is there some configuration detail that I've missed? Forms and Audit Trail are on different servers, but they are in the same domain and regularly communicate since LFServer and LFDS are on the same server that Audit Trail is on. I just can't figure out why this isn't working. 0 0 Post Link Answer SELECTED ANSWER replied on April 28, 2023 That is correct. Updated Audit Trail is required for reporting on Forms audit events. 2 0 Post Link Replies Sort by: Most popular Most popular Oldest to newest Newest t

promotionQueue.title: Audit Trail Service will not start after server reboot
promotionQueue.snippet: Audit Trail Updated May 9, 2018 Subscribe Subscribed asked on May 9, 2018 We are on Rio 9.1 & Windows Server 2012 and when I try to load Audit Trail Reporting I get the message "(Failed to connect to an IPC Port: The system cannot find the file specified.)". When trying to restart the service I get an "Error 1053: The service did not respond to the start or control request in a timely fashion". We rebooted the server after trying to restart the service and it will not start automatically or manually. We do run Windows server updates each month, so I was wondering if there are any updates that may conflict with this. Thanks! Robin 0 0 Post Link Answer SELECTED ANSWER replied on May 9, 2018 Thanks! We did a repair on Audit Trail install and that fixed it for us. 0 0 Post Link Replies Sort by: Most popular Most popular Oldest to newest Newest to oldest replied on May 9, 2018 I just install

promotionQueue.snippet: bscribed asked on May 6, 2025 We are using Quick Fields for lookups via ODBC on a PC running Office 2016 . However, after migrating to Office 365 , Quick Fields now throws an ODBC error when accessing an Excel file stored on the PC. After a quick investigation, it appears that Office 365 does not natively support ODBC , meaning we may need to either purchase a third-party ODBC driver (I’ll check the trial version) or consider reverting to a previous version. At this point, it’s still unclear whether Office 2024 has the same ODBC compatibility issue. Has anyone successfully used Quick Fields lookup with Office 365 Excel —whether stored locally on a PC or on OneDrive ? Any insights would be appreciated. Get the following error when starting the business process. It works successfully if the user account is added instead of the group to Business process security. Error Code: 0 Error Message: Access Denied. **USER** does not have permission to start the business process. Thank you, Raul Gonzalez 0 0 Post Link Answer SELECTED ANSWER replied on October 19, 2017 The Try-Catch will create 4 new tokens with this information that show up in the Catch branch.
